name: research-trail-footer
description: Collapsible bottom-of-article epistemic-frontier disclosure. Three fixed subsections: Research done / Suggested research / Open questions. Collapsed by default; summary line shows counts ('N done · N suggested · N open question(s)'). Native <details>/<summary> — no JS needed. Emits JSON-LD potentialAction nodes (SearchAction + Question). Doctrine claim #39 at article scale.
html:
Research trail — 4 done · 3 suggested · 1 open question

Research done

  • Klein 2009 — seL4 formal verification (academic source)
  • Wikipedia capability-based-security article (secondary survey)

Suggested research

  • seL4 capability-revocation 2024–2026 papers — verify transitive revocation behaviour

Open questions

  • Does the seL4 microkernel revoke capabilities transitively when a parent capability is destroyed?
placement: bottom-of-article, after See also and before References

variants.name: collapsed
variants.description: Default — summary line with counts visible; body hidden.

variants.name: suppressed
variants.description: Not rendered — article frontmatter declares research_trail: false, or all three counts are zero.

aria: Native <details>/<summary> element pair. Browsers handle keyboard (Enter/Space on summary), screen-reader announcement, and aria-expanded natively. Subsections use <h3> because the article's '## Research trail' renders as <h2>. Focus ring on summary via :focus-visible.
ai_hint: Emits JSON-LD potentialAction nodes: SearchAction for suggested-research items, Question for open-question items. LLM consumers identify article epistemic frontier without reading prose.
engine_note: Summary counts derived from frontmatter fields research_done_count / research_suggested_count / open_questions_count — render-time substitution, not author-maintained.
open_questions: §7(f) of research draft — visual weight as chrome (collapsed, muted) vs body content (expanded, same rhythm as article sections). Affects semantic.surface.layer-accent vs semantic.surface.layer token choice.
Render-time count emission — engine reads frontmatter fields directly OR counts body subsection items at render time?
